names.japanese_name: メタ重亜硫酸ナトリウム

natural_occurrence.description: A synthetic inorganic salt that serves as a disinfectant, antioxidant, and preservative. The main ingredient in Campden tablets used in wine and beer production. Part of the sulfite family (E220-E228) that release sulfur dioxide (SO₂). Known allergen causing severe reactions in sensitive individuals, especially asthmatics.

manufacturing.description: Produced by treating a solution of sodium carbonate or sodium hydroxide with excess sulfur dioxide (SO₂) gas. The resulting solution is concentrated and crystallized to yield sodium metabisulfite. Can also be made by dehydrating sodium bisulfite. The compound exists as Na₂S₂O₅ but dissociates to form sulfur dioxide in aqueous solution.

other_uses.cosmetics: Used in some cosmetic formulations as an antioxidant
other_uses.industrial: Used in water treatment, textile bleaching, paper manufacturing, photography, and mining
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Used in pharmaceutical formulations as an antioxidant and stabilizer
other_uses.household: Main ingredient in Campden tablets for home wine-making and brewing

eu.conditions: MANDATORY allergen declaration 'contains sulfites' required when total SO₂ ≥ 10 mg/kg or 10 mg/L. Temporary group ADI established with concerns for high consumers.
eu.legal_reference: Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008, Annex II; EU Directive 2003/89/EC (allergen labeling)

usa.notes: MANDATORY allergen declaration 'contains sulfites' when ≥10 ppm SO₂. Banned from fresh produce intended to be eaten raw (since 1986).

japan.japanese_regulation_name: メタ重亜硫酸ナトリウム
japan.notes: Part of sulfite group; allergen labeling mandatory

safety_evaluations.jecfa_adi: 0–0.7 mg/kg bw/day (temporary group ADI for E220-E228, expressed as SO₂)
safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: 0.7 mg SO₂ equivalents/kg bw/day (temporary group ADI for E220-E228)

regulatory_history.event: FDA bans sulfites from fresh fruits and vegetables after deaths from severe allergic reactions, particularly in restaurant salad bars
EU Directive 2003/89/EC mandates allergen labeling for sulfites when ≥10 mg/kg or mg/L
EFSA/JECFA re-evaluate sulfites; set temporary group ADI of 0.7 mg SO₂/kg bw/day citing uncertainties and concerns for high consumers

commonly_found_in: Wine and beer (main ingredient in Campden tablets)
Dried apricots, raisins, and other dried fruits
Fresh, peeled, cut, shredded, and frozen vegetables

equivalents.product: servings of dried apricots (40g at 2000mg/kg SO₂)

adi_context.source_note: Based on EFSA/JECFA temporary group ADI for sulfites (E220-E228). CRITICAL ALLERGEN WARNING: Affects ~1% of general population, 5-10% of asthmatics. Symptoms range from mild (headache, itching, hives, rashes) to severe (bronchoconstriction, wheezing, difficulty breathing, diarrhea, nausea, hypotension, anaphylaxis). Deaths have occurred. EFSA concluded current ADI generally protective BUT safety concerns exist for high consumers of sulfite-rich foods who may exceed ADI. Mandatory 'contains sulfites' warning required.
